title: argentina leuconota var. brachyphyllaria
side_effects: Argentina leuconota var. brachyphyllaria can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including skin irritation, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. In severe cases, improper use of this plant can lead to anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction. Additionally, ingesting large quantities of the plant's leaves or stems can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain due to its toxic compounds. Furthermore, using the plant for medicinal purposes without proper knowledge and caution can interact with other medications, resulting in adverse effects such as bleeding, bruising, or changes in blood pressure.
